title: Combating Halal Fraud with Species-Specific qPCR Authentication
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Halal   integrity     is     a     critical     concern     for   Muslim   consumers     worldwide  ,   requiring     strict     adherence     to     religious     guidelines     regarding     animal     species     and     processing     methods  . Food   fraud     involving     substitution     with   non-halal   animal     components     threatens     consumer     trust  ,   religious     compliance  ,   and     trade     credibility  .   Species-specific   real-  time   PCR (  qPCR  )   offers     a     powerful     solution     for     authenticating     meat     and     processed     food     ingredients     with     high     sensitivity     and     specificity  .   This     molecular     approach     can     detect     even     trace     amounts     of   non-halal DNA,   ensuring     that     fraudulent     or     contaminated     products   are   quickly     identified  .   Integrating     qPCR   testing   into   halal   certification     programs     enhances     supply     chain     transparency     and     strengthens   global   market     access     for     certified   halal   products  .   Governments     and     industry     stakeholders     must     collaborate     to     establish     standardized   testing   protocols  ,   laboratory     capacity  ,   and     regulatory     frameworks     for   halal   authentication  .
